opp.name: Marmon_Intake_ANA
opp.next_step: Validation Meeting: Paul Maynard to check VP Fadi’s calendar for a one-hour high-level demo in the next 2–3 weeks. Demo Prep: Arthur Raguette and Zycus presales to prepare demonstrations focusing on: Spend Analysis (Line-level visibility). Supplier Risk/Performance. Intake/Guided Buying. Marketplace Alignment: Follow up on how Zycus can specifically enable Marmon’s marketplace expansion to $2B.
opp.ais_why:
• On the 4/10/26 call, Marmon VP Paul Maynard said they're 'using Microsoft Copilot as a passive assistant' and explicitly want 'proactive agentic AI across procurement' — automating intake, guided buying, and tactical sourcing.
• Same call captured a plan to 'deploy agents for intake, negotiation, supplier risk and spend analysis', and Arthur live-demoed checking an invoice via Merlin Intake inside Microsoft Teams.
• Demo prep is focused on intake and guided buying aligned to a $2B marketplace target — Marmon has asked Zycus to block an hour with presales for spend analysis, supplier management, and intake demos.

sources: Paul Maynard, Avoma discovery call 10 Apr 2026
SF Next_Step__c field (marketplace $2B target)
metrics.narrative: Paul Maynard quantified the business problem on the 10 Apr discovery call: Marmon has $9 billion in annual spend across 160 businesses, but $500 million to $700 million is likely miscategorized due to algorithmic errors in their current SpendHQ tool, which provides only 5-6 data fields and aggregate-level analysis. This makes it extremely difficult to identify spend leakage and strategic savings opportunities at scale. The leverage spend team (newly restructured under VP Fadi Abu Manneh) aims to shift from firefighting to planned execution and has set a target to bring $2 billion in spend under management via their marketplace. Paul also noted that supplier risk management is in infancy and contract metadata extraction is fully manual (via the relevant stakeholder), adding touchpoints and error risk. The pain is quantified (hundreds of millions at risk) and tied to operational constraints (decentralized procurement across 1,200 global locations, multiple ERPs). The next step is to build a business case for the VP, anchored to these metrics.

sources: Paul Maynard, Avoma discovery call 10 Apr 2026
OpportunityContactRole: Paul Maynard, Manager Marketplace Operations, role=Evaluator
SF Next_Step__c 01 Jun (Paul to check VP's calendar, coordinate demo prep)
SF LastActivityDate 22 Jun 2026
champion.narrative: Paul Maynard (Manager, Marketplace Operations) is the developing champion. He initiated the discovery conversation, articulated detailed business pain and use-case priorities on the 10 Apr call, requested specific Zycus demos (Spend Analysis, Supplier Risk, Intake/Guided Buying), and committed to check VP Fadi Abu Manneh's calendar for the next validation meeting. He demonstrated access to the Decision Maker (reports directly to Fadi, building the business case for him) and is driving the internal agenda (launching 'Leveraged Spend Advocates' at each business, coordinating demo prep per Next_Step__c 01 Jun). However, he has not yet shown evidence of advocacy against competitors or pushed back on internal resistance. His engagement is active (last contact 22 Jun per LastActivityDate), but he has not introduced Zycus to other stakeholders beyond the planned VP demo. Champion strength is developing: strong access and alignment, but advocacy and multi-threading are not yet confirmed. The next gate is whether Paul successfully schedules the VP demo and whether he positions Zycus favorably in his business case to Fadi.

sources: SF Competitors__c field (Amazon Business, Coupa, SAP Ariba)
Paul Maynard, Avoma call 10 Apr 2026 (current stack: SpendHQ, Microsoft Copilot, manual contract forms; interest in agentic AI and avoiding vendor lock-in)
competition.narrative: Three competitors are named in the Salesforce Competitors__c field: Amazon Business, Coupa, and SAP Ariba. None were mentioned by name on the two Avoma calls (02 Apr internal coordination, 10 Apr discovery with Paul Maynard), so their status, threat level, and buyer sentiment are unconfirmed from call evidence. The competitive landscape is inferred from the buyer's current state: Paul stated on 10 Apr that Marmon currently uses SpendHQ for spend analysis (a lightweight tool, not a full S2P suite), Microsoft Copilot for AI assistance (passive, not agentic), and a manual Microsoft Form for contract metadata. He expressed interest in agentic AI for intake, guided buying, supplier risk, and spend analysis, and emphasized avoiding vendor lock-in. This suggests the buyer is either evaluating multiple platforms (Amazon Business for guided buying/marketplace, Coupa or Ariba for full S2P) or is early enough that the shortlist is not yet locked. The wedge is Merlin agentic AI (intake, negotiation, supplier risk agents) combined with line-level spend visibility and decentralized-org configurability (policy-driven approvals per business unit). The next demo with the relevant stakeholder (Decision Maker) is the gate to surface the active competitive shortlist and position Zycus's AI differentiation.

sources: Paul Maynard, Avoma discovery call 10 Apr 2026
OpportunityContactRoles (pain owners: Fadi Abu Manneh VP, Paul Maynard Manager, John Humphres Director, Brian Heckler Sr. Manager)
identify_pain.narrative: Paul Maynard articulated urgent operational and financial pain on the 10 Apr call. Marmon's spend analysis is constrained by SpendHQ's 5-field, aggregate-only output, making it impossible to identify $500M-$700M in miscategorized spend within a $9B budget. Supplier risk management is manual and in infancy, limiting proactive risk insights. Contract metadata extraction relies on a manual Microsoft Form, adding touchpoints and error risk. The leverage spend team has been restructured under VP Fadi Abu Manneh to standardize processes across 12 holding groups and 160 global entities, but the current toolkit forces them into firefighting mode rather than planned execution. Paul expressed frustration that 'current SpendHQ tools serve as a compass rather than a GPS' and stated the need for line-level visibility and AI-driven automation. The pain owner is VP Fadi Abu Manneh (Leveraged Spend) and his direct reports (Paul Maynard, Marketplace Ops; John Humphres, Supply Chain Director; Brian Heckler, Supplier Management). The compelling event is the marketplace expansion target ($2B under management) and the end-of-year legal system migration, both of which create urgency for a scalable, integrated procurement platform.

sources: Paul Maynard, Avoma call 10 Apr 2026 (legal moving to new contract tool end-year, multiple ERPs)
SF Current_Contract_Expiration__c (not populated)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec) have not been discussed. Paul Maynard noted on 10 Apr that Marmon uses multiple ERP systems and that legal is migrating contract governance from the current Impact system to a new tool by end-of-year, but he did not detail procurement routing, signature authority, or InfoSec requirements. The deal is in Qualified stage (early discovery), so paper process is not yet in scope. The org's decentralization (160 businesses, each with its own ERP and policies) suggests contracting may be complex and federated. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is not populated, so there is no incumbent-renewal forcing function. The VP demo and subsequent commercials phase will be the gate to surface paper-process requirements. Until then, this is a genuine knowledge gap.

economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer is not yet confirmed. VP Fadi Abu Manneh (Vice President, Leveraged Spend) is identified as the Decision Maker per OpportunityContactRole and is the senior authority Paul Maynard reports to; Paul stated on the 10 Apr call that he needs to build a business case for Fadi and will check Fadi's calendar for the next demo. Fadi has not yet joined a call, so budget authority and active sponsorship are unconfirmed. The org is highly decentralized (12 holding groups, 160 businesses), and Paul noted that the CEO maintains business autonomy but encourages inward collaboration; this suggests budget may be federated or require cross-business alignment. The planned one-hour demo with Fadi in the next 2-3 weeks (per Next_Step__c, 01 Jun) is the gate to confirm economic sponsorship. Until Fadi engages and discusses commercial scope, budget authority remains inferred from role, not confirmed from behavior.

decision_process.narrative: The process is early and emerging. Paul Maynard (Manager, Marketplace Operations) is the discovery and demo-coordination contact; he stated on 10 Apr that he must build a business case for VP Fadi Abu Manneh (Decision Maker per Contact Role) and will check Fadi's calendar for a one-hour demo in the next 2-3 weeks. This suggests a two-tier process: Paul vets and prepares, Fadi evaluates and decides. The org is highly decentralized (12 holding groups, 160 businesses, 1,200 locations), and the CEO maintains business autonomy while encouraging collaboration; this implies the decision may require cross-business buy-in or federated approval, though Paul has not yet detailed the approval chain. OpportunityContactRoles include five stakeholders (VP, Directors, Managers) across Leveraged Spend, Supply Chain, Purchasing, Marketplace Ops, and Supplier Management, indicating a committee. The Next_Step__c (01 Jun) names demo prep and marketplace alignment follow-up as the immediate gates. No mention yet of RFI/RFP, procurement channel, or legal/InfoSec gates. The next demo with Fadi will clarify the formal process.

sources: Paul Maynard, Avoma discovery call 10 Apr 2026
SF Next_Step__c 01 Jun (demo topics: Spend Analysis, Supplier Risk, Intake/Guided Buying)
SF Products__c field (iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin, Merlin Intake, ANA)
decision_criteria.narrative: Paul Maynard outlined evaluation priorities on the 10 Apr call: (1) line-level spend visibility beyond the current 5-field aggregate (SpendHQ limitation), (2) AI-driven supplier risk and performance management to mature the infancy-stage process, (3) agentic AI for intake and guided buying (moving beyond passive Microsoft Copilot to proactive agents), (4) contract metadata extraction to replace the manual Microsoft Form workflow, and (5) integration feasibility with Workday, iGlue, and multiple ERPs across 160 businesses. He emphasized starting with small pilots that evolve to production rather than siloed POCs, and expressed caution on long-term AI vendor lock-in. The buyer requested demonstrations of Spend Analysis, Supplier Risk/Performance, and Intake/Guided Buying (per Next_Step__c). These are the confirmed use-case criteria for the upcoming VP demo. Formal decision criteria (RFP/RFI scoring, bake-off structure) are not yet documented; the deal is pre-RFI, so criteria are emerging from discovery.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI Hungry. Paul Maynard's 10 Apr discovery call demonstrated strong AI appetite: he articulated dissatisfaction with passive Microsoft Copilot ('seeking proactive agentic AI'), requested AI-driven intake and guided buying to replace manual processes, asked for supplier risk and performance management automation (currently in infancy), and expressed interest in contract metadata extraction via AI to eliminate the manual Microsoft Form workflow. He emphasized avoiding vendor lock-in but framed AI as a strategic enabler, not a nice-to-have. The buyer's pain (hundreds of millions in miscategorized spend, manual supplier risk, decentralized procurement chaos) and their $2B marketplace expansion target position AI as the lever to shift from firefighting to planned execution. No AIS_Score__c on record, but the discovery conversation signals AI Hungry—buyer-initiated interest in agentic AI across multiple use cases, tied to quantified business outcomes.

north_star_verdict.headline: Early qualified deal with strong buyer engagement and clear next milestone: VP decision-maker demo scheduled within 2–3 weeks, multi-thread initiated across 5 contacts, articulated $500M+ pain in spend categorization and supplier risk, 286 days to close aligns with enterprise motion.

flags: Position Merlin agentic AI (intake negotiation agent, supplier risk agent) as the core differentiator in the VP demo—this is the wedge against Amazon Business's catalog-first guided buying, Coupa's static workflows, and Ariba's legacy platform; Paul Maynard stated on 10 Apr that they want proactive agentic AI vs passive Microsoft Copilot, so make the agents the demo centerpiece and show live automation on a Marmon-like use case (decentralized approval routing, supplier risk alert)
best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers for this deal: (1) Position Merlin agentic AI as the differentiator against Amazon Business's catalog-first model and Coupa/Ariba's static workflows—demo the intake negotiation agent and supplier risk agent with Paul and the VP to show proactive automation vs passive tools. (2) Secure multi-threaded engagement beyond Paul Maynard: the VP demo is the gate, but also reach the other four OpportunityContactRole stakeholders (John Humphres Supply Chain Director, Rob Leda Purchasing Director, Brian Heckler Supplier Management Sr. Manager) to build consensus and de-risk single-thread dependency. (3) Build the $2B marketplace business case with Paul before the VP demo: quantify how Merlin Intake + ANA guided buying accelerates time-to-contract and spend capture vs their current knowledge-center-only marketplace, and tie it to the $500M-$700M miscategorized-spend recovery opportunity—this anchors the VP conversation in ROI, not features.

competitive_position.summary: Three competitors on record (Amazon Business, Coupa, SAP Ariba per SF Competitors__c), but none mentioned on the two buyer calls, so threat level and buyer sentiment are unconfirmed. The buyer's current stack is lightweight (SpendHQ for spend analysis, Microsoft Copilot for AI, manual Microsoft Forms for contracts), suggesting they are either early in evaluation or dissatisfied with their tools and seeking a full S2P replacement. Amazon Business is the likely highest current threat if Marmon is expanding their marketplace and considering Amazon's guided-buying platform; Coupa and Ariba are the full-suite S2P incumbents but may be seen as expensive or over-engineered for Marmon's decentralized model (12 holding groups, 160 businesses). The wedge is Merlin agentic AI (intake, negotiation, supplier risk agents) + line-level spend visibility + decentralized configurability (policy-driven approvals per business). The VP demo (with Decision Maker Fadi Abu Manneh, upcoming in 2-3 weeks) is the gate to confirm the active shortlist and position Zycus's AI differentiation against the field.

competitors.how_we_win: Merlin agentic AI for intake and negotiation outpaces Amazon's catalog-first model; line-level spend visibility and supplier risk analytics are enterprise-grade vs Amazon's transactional focus; position as the AI-native S2P platform for complex, decentralized orgs vs a marketplace bolt-on

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Coupa's monolithic, expensive S2P suite is a poor fit for Marmon's decentralized 160-business model; Zycus's configurable, business-unit-level policy engine (demo: intake approval routing per business) and lower TCO are the wedge; Merlin agentic AI (negotiation, supplier risk) is differentiated vs Coupa's static workflows

ai_positioning_strength.score: Strong opportunity, not yet delivered to Decision Maker
ai_positioning_strength.summary: Strong AI positioning opportunity. Paul Maynard explicitly stated on the 10 Apr call that Marmon is using Microsoft Copilot as a passive assistant and is seeking proactive agentic AI for intake, guided buying, supplier risk insights, and contract metadata extraction. He emphasized the desire to automate manual workflows (the manual Microsoft Form for contract metadata, the 5-field SpendHQ limitation) and to avoid vendor lock-in. This maps directly to Merlin's agentic AI roadmap (intake negotiation agent, supplier risk agent, spend analysis agent). However, the AI story has not yet been delivered to the Decision Maker (VP Fadi Abu Manneh) or the broader stakeholder group; the upcoming VP demo (next 2-3 weeks per Next_Step__c) is the gate to position Merlin as the differentiator. The deal is not under-positioned (Paul clearly understands the AI value prop), but the AI narrative must be elevated to the VP level and tied to quantified business impact ($500M-$700M spend recovery, $2B marketplace target) to secure sponsorship. No AIS score on record, so appetite is inferred from discovery.

items.quote: Using Microsoft Copilot as passive assistant; seeking proactive agentic AI across procurement
items.criterion: Agentic AI for intake, guided buying, supplier risk, contract metadata extraction (vs passive Microsoft Copilot)

items.quote: Desire for advanced spend analysis with line-level data beyond the current five fields in Spendhq; current tools serve as a compass rather than a GPS
items.criterion: Line-level spend visibility beyond current 5-field SpendHQ aggregate analysis

items.quote: Interest in supplier risk and performance management capabilities to mature the current infancy-stage process
items.criterion: Supplier risk and performance management (currently in infancy, manual)

items.quote: Organization is extremely decentralized across 12 holding groups, 160 businesses, and 1,200 global locations; multiple ERP systems and policies per business complicate centralized processes
items.source: Paul Maynard, Avoma call 10 Apr (fit challenge: must demo business-unit-level config)
items.criterion: Decentralized configurability: policy-driven approvals per business unit across 160 businesses

items.quote: Emphasis on starting small pilots that evolve into production rather than siloed POCs

items.quote: Caution on long-term AI commitments; avoid vendor lock-in
items.source: Paul Maynard, Avoma call 10 Apr (address in commercial model)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong alignment on AI-native procurement, spend visibility, and decentralized configurability. Paul Maynard's 10 Apr discovery call priorities (line-level spend analysis, agentic AI for intake/guided buying/supplier risk, contract metadata extraction, small-pilot-to-production approach, avoiding vendor lock-in) map directly to Zycus's Merlin roadmap and modular S2P architecture. The buyer's decentralized org structure (12 holding groups, 160 businesses, multiple ERPs) is a fit challenge: Zycus must demonstrate policy-driven approval configurability per business unit and multi-ERP integration feasibility. The $2B marketplace expansion target is a strategic fit for Merlin Intake + ANA (guided buying), but the buyer's current marketplace is a knowledge center, not a transactional platform, so Zycus must show how to embed procure-to-pay workflows. No exposed misalignment yet; the VP demo is the gate to confirm fit on AI maturity, integration complexity, and commercial model.

hard.ais_why:
• On the 4/10/26 call, Marmon VP Paul Maynard said they're 'using Microsoft Copilot as a passive assistant' and explicitly want 'proactive agentic AI across procurement' — automating intake, guided buying, and tactical sourcing.
• Same call captured a plan to 'deploy agents for intake, negotiation, supplier risk and spend analysis', and Arthur live-demoed checking an invoice via Merlin Intake inside Microsoft Teams.
• Demo prep is focused on intake and guided buying aligned to a $2B marketplace target — Marmon has asked Zycus to block an hour with presales for spend analysis, supplier management, and intake demos.
hard.next_step:
• Validation Meeting: Paul Maynard to check VP Fadi’s calendar for a one-hour high-level demo in the next 2–3 weeks.

  • Demo Prep: Arthur Raguette and Zycus presales to prepare demonstrations focusing on:

    1. Spend Analysis (Line-level visibility).

    2. Supplier Risk/Performance.

    3. Intake/Guided Buying.

  • Marketplace Alignment: Follow up on how Zycus can specifically enable Marmon’s marketplace expansion to $2B.
